title: PaymentIntent.confirm() now requires return_url in all redirect flows
summary: Starting with the 2026-05-01 API version, the confirm() method on PaymentIntent requires the return_url parameter whenever a redirect may occur. Previously, omitting return_url caused unpredictable behavior in some payment methods; this change enforces explicit handling. Applications that call confirm() without return_url will receive a 400 error with code missing_required_param.
migration_hint: Add return_url to all confirm() calls. For server-side confirmation flows that never redirect, pass return_url: null explicitly to confirm intent.
